name: golang-patterns
description: Evaluations for golang-patterns skill trigger accuracy

evals.name: trigger-positive-go-code
evals.description: Skill should trigger on Go code writing, reviewing, or refactoring requests

prompts.input: Write a worker pool in Go using goroutines and channels
prompts.reason: Writing new Go code with concurrency patterns

prompts.input: Review this Go code for idiomatic patterns and best practices
prompts.reason: Reviewing Go code for idiomatic patterns

prompts.input: Refactor this Go package to use dependency injection instead of global state
prompts.reason: Refactoring Go code to follow patterns covered by the skill

prompts.input: How should I structure error handling in this Go service?
prompts.reason: Go error handling patterns are a core part of the skill

prompts.input: Design the package structure for a new Go HTTP service following standard layout
prompts.reason: Designing Go package/module structure matches skill activation criteria

evals.name: trigger-negative-non-go
evals.description: Skill should NOT trigger for non-Go code requests

prompts.input: Write a Python async function to fetch data from an API
How do I handle errors in TypeScript with try/catch?

prompts.reason: Conceptual explanation of Go concepts, not actively writing or reviewing Go code
